name: Mandayona from Baides — circular by the Camino del Cid
name_original: Mandayona desde Baides — circular por el Camino del Cid
region_intro: No traffic touring cycling routes around Baides offer access to the natural and historical landscapes of the Guadalajara province in Spain. The region features varied terrain, including river valleys, hills, and areas of natural parkland. Routes often traverse rural roads and paths, providing views of local architecture and natural features. The area is characterized by its connection to the Barranco del Río Dulce Natural Park, offering a mix of open spaces and more sheltered sections. Best no traffic touring cycling routes around Baides The most popular no traffic touring cycling route is Castillo de Guijosa from Sigüenza — circular through the Parque Natural del Barranco del Río Dulce, a 26.0 miles (41.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 5 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through natural parkland. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Sigüenza – Sigüenza Cathedral loop from Mandayona, a difficult 33.4 miles (53.7 km) path. This route includes substantial climbs and passes by the historic Sigüenza Cathedral. Local touring cyclists also love the Pelegrina Castle – Barranco del Río Dulce loop from Castillo de Pelegrina, a 15.8 miles (25.4 km) trail leading through the Barranco del Río Dulce, often completed in about 2 hours 24 minutes. Touring cycling around Baides is defined by river valleys, historic castles, and the natural beauty of the Barranco del Río Dulce. The network offers options for different ability levels, from shorter city loops to longer, more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Baides are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Baides's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no-traffic touring cycling routes are available around Baides?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;5&lt;/strong&gt; dedicated no-traffic touring cycling routes available in the area around Baides. These routes offer varying distances and difficulties, focusing on quiet roads and paths to ensure a peaceful cycling experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on these no-traffic touring routes?
A: The terrain varies, but many routes feature a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, often following river valleys or old railway lines (Vías Verdes, though none are directly listed in the routes provided, the concept is relevant for 'no traffic'). For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1186985208/castillo-de-guijosa-from-sigueenza-circular-through-the-parque-natural-del-barranco-del-rio-dulce" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Guijosa from Sigüenza&lt;/a&gt; route involves significant elevation changes, indicating hilly sections, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1187056870/sigueenza-city-tour-circular-through-the-city" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sigüenza City Tour&lt;/a&gt; is flatter within the city itself.

Q: Are there any circular no-traffic touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point without needing a return trip. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1186985208/castillo-de-guijosa-from-sigueenza-circular-through-the-parque-natural-del-barranco-del-rio-dulce" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Guijosa from Sigüenza&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1187056870/sigueenza-city-tour-circular-through-the-city" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sigüenza City Tour&lt;/a&gt;, both designed as loops.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I can see along these routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near significant natural and historical sites. You can explore the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5306728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Dulce Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7497829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges of the Dulce River Ravine&lt;/a&gt;, or visit historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3967563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sigüenza Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3399704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pelegrina Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1186985208/castillo-de-guijosa-from-sigueenza-circular-through-the-parque-natural-del-barranco-del-rio-dulce" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Guijosa from Sigüenza&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, takes you through the Parque Natural del Barranco del Río Dulce.

Q: Are there any family-friendly no-traffic cycling routes?
A: Yes, for families looking for an easier ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1187056870/sigueenza-city-tour-circular-through-the-city" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sigüenza City Tour&lt;/a&gt; is classified as 'easy' with minimal elevation gain, making it suitable for a relaxed family outing. It's a great way to explore the historic town center without significant traffic concerns.

Q: What is the best season to cycle around Baides for no-traffic routes?
A: While specific regional climate data for Baides is limited, generally, spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) offer the most pleasant cycling conditions in this part of Spain. Temperatures are milder, and the natural landscapes are often at their most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cold, especially at higher elevations.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the no-traffic routes in this area?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.33 stars&lt;/strong&gt;. Reviewers often praise the opportunity to explore historical sites like Sigüenza and Pelegrina castles, as well as the natural beauty of the Río Dulce Gorge, all while enjoying quiet roads away from heavy traffic.

Q: Where can I find parking for these no-traffic touring cycling routes?
A: For routes starting in towns like Sigüenza, you can typically find public parking facilities within the town. For routes that begin in smaller villages or natural areas, look for designated parking areas near trailheads or points of interest. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat along the no-traffic routes?
A: Routes that pass through or start in towns and villages like Sigüenza or Mandayona will offer opportunities for cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel. For longer routes that venture into more natural or remote areas, it's recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks, as amenities might be sparse.

Q: Are dogs allowed on these no-traffic touring cycling routes?
A: While specific regulations for each route can vary, generally, dogs are permitted on many outdoor trails in Spain, especially if kept on a leash. However, if a route passes through a protected natural park, such as the Parque Natural del Barranco del Río Dulce, it's always best to check the specific park regulations regarding pets to ensure compliance.

Q: What is the difficulty level of the no-traffic touring routes?
A: The routes around Baides offer a range of difficulties. There is one 'easy' route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1187056870/sigueenza-city-tour-circular-through-the-city" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sigüenza City Tour&lt;/a&gt;. There is also one 'moderate' route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31502477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pelegrina Castle – Barranco del Río Dulce loop from Castillo de Pelegrina&lt;/a&gt;. The remaining &lt;strong&gt;4 routes&lt;/strong&gt; are classified as 'difficult', featuring more significant elevation changes and longer distances, suitable for experienced touring cyclists.
tips: Mandayona sits next to the Camino del Cid. There are ancient caves to visit and the ruins of a historic castle. You can also visit the Barranco Del Rio Dulcen Natural Park Interpretation Center for more information about the area.

Where the Henares River flows into the Salado River, is the small town of Baides. You can take a local train into the city and start your journey. There's even a train museum if that's something you're into.
